name: Terminal49
description: Terminal49 is an API and dashboard for ocean-container shipment tracking, integrating with every major ocean carrier and U.S./Canadian marine terminals to standardize vessel schedules, container milestones, demurrage/per-diem risk, and customs holds into one feed.

disruptionMechanisms: Replaces manual carrier-website and EDI tracking with a unified API
Standardizes data across fragmented carriers and terminals
attributes.problemStatement: Importers, forwarders, and 3PLs must manually check dozens of carrier websites and portals to track ocean containers, with no standardized, real-time way to see milestones, demurrage risk, or customs holds across carriers and terminals.
attributes.productDescription: API and dashboard that standardizes ocean-carrier and marine-terminal data into one searchable feed covering vessel schedules, container milestones, demurrage/per-diem alerts, and customs status; the company states its integrations cover about 98% of global ocean freight and container traffic.

founders.name: Akshay Dodeja
founders.background: Co-founded Terminal49 in 2015; exact current title unconfirmed in public sources.

founders.name: Philipp Gutheim
founders.background: Co-founded Terminal49 in 2015 alongside Dodeja; exact current title unconfirmed in public sources.

name: Nira Energy
description: Nira builds software that automates transmission interconnection and grid-planning studies for renewable, storage, and data-center developers, replacing manual engineering studies with claimed 50-100x faster analysis.

disruptionMechanisms: Automates manual engineering studies (50-100x speed claim)
Replaces spreadsheet/PSS-E based transmission planning
attributes.problemStatement: Renewable, storage, and data-center developers face years-long, manual, opaque interconnection studies run by a scarce pool of transmission planning engineers, creating a major bottleneck to U.S. grid buildout.
attributes.productDescription: Cloud software that ingests utility and RTO/ISO grid data to identify the lowest-cost, fastest interconnection points for a proposed project; the company reports support across more than 500 GW of interconnection studies and identification of more than $3 billion in cost discrepancies for customers.

founders.name: Chris Ariante
founders.background: Founded Nira in 2021 and led the company to profitability from its first year; identified as CEO in Latitude Media and Energize Capital coverage.

founders.name: Andy Chen
founders.background: Previously worked at Palantir before co-founding Nira Energy, per Latitude Media.

name: Bayou Energy
description: Bayou Energy provides a developer API that aggregates and standardizes customer utility bills, interval usage, and account data across thousands of regional U.S. electric and gas utilities for clean-energy software platforms.

disruptionMechanisms: Replaces bespoke utility-website integrations with a unified API
Removes per-integration engineering cost for clean-energy platforms
attributes.problemStatement: Clean-energy software companies must otherwise build and maintain their own screen-scraping or login integrations against thousands of fragmented, non-standardized U.S. utility websites to access customer bill and usage data.
attributes.productDescription: Developer API that returns standardized utility bill, interval-usage, and account data typically within 60 seconds of a customer connecting their utility account, with a stated 95%+ reliability rate across major U.S. utilities; public pricing is $24 per connected meter per year with the first ten meters free.

founders.name: James Gordey
founders.background: Co-founded Bayou Energy in 2021; company went through Y Combinator's Winter 2022 batch. Exact current title unconfirmed.

founders.name: Joris Van Hecke
founders.background: Co-founded Bayou Energy alongside Gordey. Exact current title unconfirmed.

name: Vooma
description: Vooma builds AI agents (Vooma Quote, Vooma Build, Vooma Voice) that automate the repetitive email, data-entry, and phone communication tasks embedded in truckload freight brokerage and carrier operations.

disruptionMechanisms: Automates manual broker/carrier communication across email, text, and voice
Reduces the historically linear headcount-to-volume scaling of freight brokerage
attributes.problemStatement: Freight brokerage and carrier operations rely on large teams manually drafting rate quotes, entering load data, and handling booking calls, which scales headcount roughly linearly with load volume and caps margin.
attributes.productDescription: Multi-channel AI agent platform for freight brokers and carriers: Vooma Quote identifies rate requests and drafts responses, Vooma Build automates load-order data entry, and Vooma Voice handles inbound booking and scheduling calls; customers include Echo, MODE, Arrive Logistics, and NFI.

founders.name: Jesse Buckingham
founders.background: Stanford graduate; former CEO of ASG LogisTech, a logistics operator, prior to co-founding Vooma.

founders.name: Mike Carter
founders.background: Stanford graduate; former founding engineer at Kodiak Robotics, an autonomous-trucking company, prior to co-founding Vooma.

name: Lithos Carbon
description: Lithos Carbon removes atmospheric CO2 through enhanced rock weathering, spreading crushed basalt on farmland and selling registry-certified carbon-removal credits under offtake agreements with corporate buyers.

disruptionMechanisms: New scalable carbon-removal pathway using existing farmland
Registry-certified MRV for a historically hard-to-verify removal method
attributes.problemStatement: Gigaton-scale, durable carbon removal requires pathways that do not depend on new energy-intensive industrial infrastructure or new land use, and existing enhanced-weathering approaches lack rigorous, registry-certifiable measurement of actual CO2 removed.
attributes.productDescription: Applies crushed basalt to farmland, where weathering reactions convert atmospheric CO2 into dissolved bicarbonate while releasing soil nutrients; the company measures and verifies removal through its own MRV process and sells resulting credits, and in December 2025 delivered 5,160 registry-certified tons in what it described as the world's largest enhanced-rock-weathering issuance to date.

founders.name: Mary Yap
founders.background: Identified as CEO and company spokesperson across public reporting, including the Frontier offtake announcement.

founders.name: Noah Planavsky
founders.background: Academic geochemistry/earth-science background; exact current institutional affiliation not independently confirmed.

founders.name: Chris Reinhard
founders.background: Academic geochemistry/earth-science background; exact current institutional affiliation not independently confirmed.

name: Paces
description: Paces is a GIS and AI-driven data platform (Project Search, Permitting Predictor) that helps renewable-energy developers screen and de-risk potential project sites using consolidated spatial, zoning, permitting, interconnection, and environmental data.

disruptionMechanisms: Replaces manual multi-jurisdiction GIS/permitting research with AI-driven due diligence
Compresses siting due diligence from months to minutes (company claim)
attributes.problemStatement: Renewable developers must manually cross-reference zoning maps, environmental data, and permitting history across thousands of U.S. jurisdictions with no common schema before committing capital to a potential project site.
attributes.productDescription: Project Search lets developers screen sites against custom parameters using consolidated spatial, zoning, permitting, interconnection, and environmental data; Permitting Predictor analyzes local, state, and federal zoning/permitting risk. Disclosed customers include EDF Renewables, AES, and Third Pillar Solar.

founders.name: James Mcwalter
founders.background: Identified as co-founder and CEO across multiple financing reports (ESG Dive, Utility Dive, ESG Today); company founded 2022, headquartered in Brooklyn, NY.

founders.name: Charles Bai
founders.background: Identified alongside Mcwalter as co-founder in company and press materials; exact current title unconfirmed.

name: Verse
description: Verse builds Aria, a SaaS platform that helps corporations set, plan, procure, and manage clean-energy goals, from renewable energy credit purchases to hourly-matched carbon-free-energy portfolios spanning PPAs and on-site assets.

disruptionMechanisms: Replaces broker/consultant-led clean-power procurement with software
AI-driven portfolio optimization for clean-energy contracts
attributes.problemStatement: Corporate clean-power procurement is largely a manual, broker- and consultant-driven process for structuring PPAs and REC purchases, with limited software tooling to continuously optimize a multi-year clean-energy portfolio for cost and emissions.
attributes.productDescription: Aria is a SaaS platform using generative AI to help customers define clean-energy targets and plan, procure, and manage portfolios of clean-energy assets; the company states it can help reduce electricity costs by 10% or more while eliminating Scope 2 emissions. First disclosed customer was Climeworks.

founders.name: Seyed Madaeni
founders.background: Identified as co-founder in Series A and seed financing press releases; company founded 2022. Exact current title unconfirmed.

founders.name: Matt Penfold
founders.background: Identified as co-founder alongside Madaeni in company financing materials. Exact current title unconfirmed.

name: Texture
description: Texture is a grid software platform providing utilities and cooperatives a single view of every meter, device, and data source on their network, with grid-visibility (outage detection, transformer load tracking) and grid-operations (load shifting, asset dispatch, DER coordination) capabilities.

disruptionMechanisms: Unifies fragmented utility device and data sources into one operating layer
Extends modern DER-management capability to underserved co-ops via the NRTC channel
attributes.goToMarket: Direct enterprise sales plus channel partnership (NRTC)
attributes.problemStatement: Utilities, especially smaller cooperatives, lack a unified view of meters, devices, and distributed energy resources across fragmented OEM systems, and legacy DERMS vendors are built for large investor-owned utilities, leaving co-ops underserved.
attributes.productDescription: Platform connecting to any meter, device, or data source for real-time monitoring, outage detection, transformer load tracking, and DER coordination; integrates with Tesla, FranklinWH, Honeywell, Ecobee, SolarEdge, Leap Energy, and WattTime, and is SOC 2 Type I/II certified. Disclosed customers include Vermont Electric Cooperative and sonnen, with a distribution partnership through NRTC covering 850 utility co-ops.

founders.name: Sanjiv Sanghavi
founders.background: Identified by name and title in the company's own Series A blog post.

founders.name: Additional co-founders (undisclosed full names)
founders.background: Company's Series A post references two additional co-founders by first name only ("Nick" and "Victor"); full names and titles not independently verified. Confirm in diligence.

name: Cargado
description: Cargado operates an invite-only, carrier-vetted load board for cross-border U.S.-Mexico trucking freight, connecting brokers, 3PLs, and freight forwarders with a personally vetted network of cross-border trucking companies.

disruptionMechanisms: Digitizes phone/email-based cross-border freight brokering
Adds a carrier-vetting trust layer absent from generic load boards
attributes.problemStatement: U.S.-Mexico cross-border trucking freight is underserved by modern digital marketplaces relative to domestic U.S. freight, and carrier legitimacy/customs compliance are harder to verify across the border, creating fraud and reliability risk for brokers.
attributes.productDescription: Invite-only load board where brokers, 3PLs, and freight forwarders post cross-border loads and receive bids from a vetted network of cross-border trucking companies; at its Series A the company reported more than 200 broker/3PL/forwarder customers and more than 650 vetted carriers within about six months of public launch.

founders.name: Matt Silver
founders.background: Identified as co-founder in FreightWaves coverage and company materials; company founded January 2024. Exact current title unconfirmed.

founders.name: Rylan Hawkins
founders.background: Identified as co-founder alongside Silver in FreightWaves coverage and company materials. Exact current title unconfirmed.

name: Isometric
description: Isometric operates Certify, an AI-agent-driven certification platform, alongside a public carbon-removal registry, verifying carbon-removal and (increasingly) broader industrial-sustainability claims using sensor, satellite, supply-chain, and lab data.

disruptionMechanisms: AI-accelerated certification (12 months to hours, company claim)
Buyer-funded model removing the supplier-paid conflict of interest common in legacy carbon registries
attributes.problemStatement: The voluntary carbon market's credibility has suffered because project developers historically paid for their own verification, and manual certification processes take up to 12 months, slowing both project financing and buyer confidence.
attributes.productDescription: Certify deploys AI agents to ingest and cross-check the data underlying a certification claim (sensor readings, satellite imagery, supply-chain records, lab results), flagging inconsistencies and escalating cases needing expert review; the public registry gives buyers and suppliers access to project data, protocols, and issued certificates. Started with carbon removal (biochar, enhanced weathering, direct air capture, reforestation, ocean alkalinity enhancement) and is expanding into industrial certification (steel, cement, RECs, low-carbon fuels). Disclosed customers/counterparties include Microsoft, Boeing, JPMorgan Chase, and Anglo American, with more than 16 million tonnes of carbon removal contracted across 200+ projects.

founders.name: Eamon Jubbawy
founders.background: Previously co-founded Onfido, a digital-identity verification company that verified over a billion identities before a $650m acquisition by Entrust.

founders.name: Jennifer Wilcox
founders.background: Former Principal Deputy Assistant Secretary at the U.S. Department of Energy; published foundational carbon-dioxide-removal research.

founders.name: Stacy Kauk
founders.background: Former Head of Sustainability at Shopify; founding member of the Frontier carbon-removal offtake coalition.
